label: 53301 Definitions

L. 97–31, § 12(92)(A)95 Stat. 161Aug. 6, 198153301(a) (2)(B)46 App.:1161().o, title V, § 511(), as added , ; , , .June 29, 1936, ch. 858July 17, 1952, ch. 939, § 1466 Stat. 764Pub. L. 97–31, § 12(92)(A)95 Stat. 161oAug. 6, 198153301(b)46 App.:1161(m)., title V, § 511(m), as added , .June 29, 1936, ch. 858Oct. 10, 1940, ch. 84954 Stat. 1108In subsection (a)(2)(A)(i), the words “constructed with a construction-differential subsidy under title V of the Merchant Marine Act, 1936, or constructed with financing or a financing guarantee under chapter 537 or 575 of this title” are substituted for “the construction of which has been financed under subchapters V or VII of this chapter, or the construction of which has been aided by a mortgage insured under subchapter XI of this chapter” because of the reorganization of the material and the omission from the revised title of the provisions relating to the construction-differential subsidy program.Subsection (a)(2)(B) is substituted for the source provision to state more directly that a vessel described in the source provision is a new vessel for purposes of this chapter.

children.heading: .—Construction contract
children.label: 1 .—Construction contract
children.text: The term “construction contract” includes, for a taxpayer constructing a new vessel in a shipyard owned by that taxpayer, an agreement between the taxpayer and the Secretary of Transportation for that construction containing provisions the Secretary considers advisable to carry out this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: constructed in the United States after , constructed with a construction-differential subsidy under title V of the Merchant Marine Act, 1936, or constructed with financing or a financing guarantee under chapter 537 or 575 of this title;December 31, 1939

children.label: ii
children.text: documented or agreed with the Secretary to be documented under the laws of the United States; and

children.label: I
children.text: of a type, size, and speed that the Secretary determines is suitable for use on the high seas or Great Lakes in carrying out this subtitle, but not less than 2,000 gross tons or less than 12 knots speed unless the Secretary certifies in each case that a vessel of lesser tonnage or speed is desirable for use by the United States Government in case of war or national emergency; or

children.label: II
children.text: constructed to replace a vessel bought or requisitioned by the Government; and

children.label: B
children.text: a vessel reconstructed or reconditioned for use only on the Great Lakes, including the Saint Lawrence River and Gulf, if the Secretary finds that the reconstruction or reconditioning will promote the objectives of this subtitle.

children.heading: .—Additional Tax-Related Terms
children.label: b .—Additional Tax-Related Terms
children.text: Other terms used in this chapter have the same meaning as in chapter 1 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (26 U.S.C. ch. 1).
